title: stravadium album
intro: Stravadium album, also known as Garden Straw or Wild Straw, is a type of flowering plant in the family Poaceae. It is a perennial grass that grows in wet meadows and along streams, and is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The medicinal properties of Stravadium album include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat a range of health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system, and it is often used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and salves.
